zh.lifeSummary: 當William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland於1108年1月出生在英國諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆時，他的父親Aldhelm Montaigne13歲，母親Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate14歲。他於1130年與Juliana Daisnel在英國諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。他於1171年登記服兵役。他於1189年被列為sheriff of the county of cumberland。他去世於1198年在英國諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆享年90歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 迪拉利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland于1108年1月出生在英国諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆时，他的父亲Aldhelm Montaigne13岁，母亲Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate14岁。他于1130年与Juliana Daisnel在英国諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆结婚。他们至少是7个儿子的父母。他于1171年登记服兵役。他于1189年被列为sheriff of the county of cumberland。他去世于1198年在英国諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆享年90岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 迪拉利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: When William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland was born in January 1108, in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England, his father, Aldhelm Montaigne, was 13 and his mother, Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate, was 14. He married Juliana Daisnel in 1130, in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. He registered for military service in 1171. In 1189, his occupation is listed as sheriff of the county of cumberland. He died in 1198, in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England, at the age of 90, and was buried in County Tipperary, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland est né en janvier 1108, à Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Aldhelm Montaigne, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Juliana Daisnel en 1130, à Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1171. En 1189, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of the county of cumberland. Il est décédé en 1198, à Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 90 ans, et a été enterré à County Tipperary, Irlande.

de.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland im Januar 1108 in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Aldhelm Montaigne 13 und seine Mutter Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate 14. Er hat 1130 in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England Juliana Daisnel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne. Er hat sich 1171 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1189 ist sein Beruf als sheriff of the county of cumberland aufgeführt. Er starb 1198 im Alter von 90 Jahren in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England und wurde in County Tipperary, Irland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Irelandが1108年1月にイギリスノーフォルクブルグ ネクスト エイルシャムで生まれた時，父Aldhelm Montaigneは13歳，母Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgateは14歳であった。1130年にイギリスノーフォルクブルグ ネクスト エイルシャムでJuliana Daisnelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子の親であった。彼は1171年に兵役登録をした。1189年，職業はsheriff of the county of cumberlandと記録されている。彼は1198年にイギリスノーフォルクブルグ ネクスト エイルシャムで90歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ティップエラリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 버그h 니스트 아이샤
ko.lifeSummary: William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland(이)가 1108년 1월 영국 노르폴크 버그h 니스트 아이샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Aldhelm Montaigne(은)는 13세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1130년 영국 노르폴크 버그h 니스트 아이샤에서 Juliana Daisnel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1171년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1189년, 그의 직업은 sheriff of the county of cumberland이었다. 그는 1198년 영국 노르폴크 버그h 니스트 아이샤에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 티페라리에 묻혔다.

pt.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland nasceu em janeiro de 1108, em Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Aldhelm Montaigne, tinha 13 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Juliana Daisnel em 1130, em Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1171. Em 1189, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of the county of cumberland. Ele faleceu em 1198, em Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 90 anos, e foi sepultado em County Tipperary, Irlanda.

ru.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland родился январь 1108 г. в местности бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Aldhelm Montaigne было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Juliana Daisnel в 1130 г. в местности бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. В 1171 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1189 годуработал по профессии sheriff of the county of cumberland. Он умер в 1198 г. в местности бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 90 лет и был похоронен в местности коунти типпирэри, Ирландия.

es.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Fitzaldelm de Burgh Chief Justice of Ireland nació en enero de 1108, en Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Aldhelm Montaigne, tenía 13 años y su mamá, Mrs. Aldhelm de Burgate, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Juliana Daisnel en 1130, en Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1171. En 1189, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of the county of cumberland. Murió en 1198, en Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 90 años, y fue sepultado en County Tipperary, Irlanda.
